title: Google Trends: Search Volume Uptick – 'Weather Warning Wales'
evidence: Google Trends data shows a modest uptick in search interest for 'weather warning Wales' and 'Met Office Wales rain' across England and Wales from mid-afternoon Thursday. Volume is within normal pre-weather-event range. No clear geographic concentration.

assessment.justification: Minor national search volume uptick consistent with Met Office warning publication. Dispersed geographically with no specific concentration in the area of interest. Baseline proxy signal only.
assessment.synthesis: Minor national search uptick for Welsh weather warnings on Thursday afternoon. Standard pre-event proxy signal. No geographic concentration or escalation.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Low confidence. Baseline proxy signal. National dispersion. Pre-event noise.

title: Google Trends: 'A40 flooding' and 'M4 surface water' Searches Rising in South Wales
evidence: Google Trends shows an increase in location-resolved search queries for 'A40 flooding', 'M4 surface water Wales', and 'Traffic Wales road closures' originating from postcode areas in South Wales and the Welsh Marches between 06:00 and 08:00 Friday. Volume trending above the average for a Friday morning.

assessment.justification: Location-resolved search queries for SE Wales road conditions showing early-morning uptick. Consistent with Traffic Wales advisories already in place. Proxy signal corroborating developing road disruption pattern.
assessment.synthesis: Early morning search volume for SE Wales road conditions and flooding queries is elevated and geographically concentrated in South Wales. Proxy corroboration for emerging road impact pattern.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Low confidence. Geographic concentration emerging. Proxy corroboration of road impact pattern.

title: Google Trends: 'M5 conditions' and 'Devon flooding' Searches Elevated
evidence: Google Trends data shows elevated search interest for 'M5 conditions Devon', 'A30 flooding' and 'Devon roads closed' from postcode areas in Devon, Somerset, and Cornwall between 06:30 and 08:00. Consistent with National Highways advisories for the SW England road network.

assessment.justification: Geographic concentration of SW England road queries consistent with National Highways M5 and A30 advisories. Peripheral to primary area of interest. Part of the broader early-morning proxy signal picture.
assessment.synthesis: SW England road condition searches elevated among Devon and Somerset users. Peripheral proxy signal consistent with early-morning road impacts in the southwest.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Low confidence. Peripheral location. Consistent with SW England road impact pattern.

title: Google Trends: 'A465 closure', 'Abergavenny flooding' – Sharp Search Spike in SE Wales
evidence: Google Trends records a sharp spike in search queries for 'A465 closed', 'Abergavenny flooding', 'River Usk flood warning' and 'M4 Newport closed' from postcode areas in Southeast Wales, Gloucestershire, and Herefordshire. Volume significantly above baseline for this time of day and day of week.

assessment.justification: Sharp, geographically concentrated search spike for SE Wales flooding and closure queries. Geographic distribution of searchers in SE Wales, Gloucestershire, and Herefordshire mirrors the primary impact zone. Strongly corroborates official closure data.
assessment.synthesis: Sharp search spike for A465 and Abergavenny flooding queries geographically concentrated in SE Wales and adjacent counties. Proxy signal strongly corroborates the official closure and flooding picture emerging from the primary impact zone.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. Sharp concentrated spike. Geographic distribution consistent with event. Proxy corroboration strong.

title: Google Trends: 'M4 Newport closed' – National Peak in Search Volume
evidence: Google Trends records a national peak in search interest for 'M4 Newport closed', 'M4 closure diversion', and 'Newport flooding' following the Traffic Wales closure confirmation. Searches originating from across England and Wales, concentrated in SE Wales, SW England, and the West Midlands. The M4 closure is the dominant search topic nationally.

assessment.justification: National search peak for M4 Newport closure queries is a strong proxy corroboration of the strategic significance of this closure. National geographic distribution of searchers reflects the cross-border impact. Dominant search topic indicator suggests major public awareness.
assessment.synthesis: M4 Newport closure becomes the dominant national search topic, with queries concentrated but not limited to SE Wales. The search volume scale is a strong proxy indicator of the event's strategic and national significance.
assessment.confidenceStatement: Medium confidence. National search peak. Strategic significance of M4 closure strongly reflected in proxy data.
